summary: The recommended maximum safe dosage ofarticaine for an average adult based on common medical practices would be around \(\textbf{(5-6)}\) milligrams per kilogram of bodyweight; this ensures efficacy while minimizing risk of toxicity or adverse effects within regulatory standards established by healthcare authorities such as ASRA (American Society Regional Anesthesia). This value reflects current best-practice recommendations but may vary slightly depending upon specific population characteristics including age, health status among others not provided here which could influence actual dosing decisions made clinically at individual level after thorough assessment. To ensure safety always consult latest official drug monographs from reliable sources before prescribing any medication especially those used frequently due their wide availability across different regions globally where slight variations might exist locally regarding labeling instructions etc., thus making precise determination challenging sometimes even amongst experienced clinicians themselves when dealing complex cases involving multiple interacting factors simultaneously influencing final therapeutic choices available during treatment planning phases conducted routinely
